reasoning: The Lean statement `HasSum (λ n => ((-1)^n / (2n)!) * x^(2n)) (cos x)` asserts that the partial sums of the series converge to `cos x`, which captures both claims in the informal statement: (i) the series converges for all real x, and (ii) its sum equals cos(x). The lemma takes `(x : ℝ)` as an argument, so it is universally quantified over all real x. The term `((-1)^n / (2n)!) * x^(2n)` is algebraically the same as Mathlib's canonical `(-1)^n * x^(2n) / (2n)!` (cf. `Real.hasSum_cos`). The summation index `n : ℕ` starts at 0 as in the informal sum. No hypotheses are dropped or added, and the types match.
